title: Colorado Judge: ICE 'Materially Violated' Court Order; Orders Mandatory Training Within 45 Days
excerpt: U.S. District Judge R. Brooke Jackson issued a 60-page ruling finding that ICE had 'materially violated' his November 2025 preliminary injunction on warrantless arrests in Colorado, finding that not one of 36 arrest forms reviewed complied with the court's documentation requirements. He ordered ICE to develop a mandatory training program within two weeks and barred untrained agents from making warrantless arrests.
content:
On May 12, 2026, Senior U.S. District Court Judge R. Brooke Jackson issued a sweeping 60-page enforcement order finding that Immigration and Customs Enforcement had materially violated his November 2025 preliminary injunction governing warrantless arrests in Colorado.

  • Jackson found that ICE had continued to make warrantless arrests without individualized, pre-arrest probable cause determinations of flight risk in violation of federal law and his prior court order.
  • The judge found that not one of the 36 arrest forms (I-213s) reviewed by the court complied with his documentation mandate: 'not one of the 36 I-213s that have been produced pursuant to the PI Order complies with this mandate.'
  • At hearings in March 2026, ICE officers testified that they did not understand the rules governing warrantless arrests, had not read relevant communications, and were generally unaware of what was required of them. The Denver field office's assistant director acknowledged dozens of new officers had been hired recently and that training hours had been cut.
  • ICE was ordered to develop a compliant training program within 14 days, and to train every officer authorized to make warrantless arrests in Colorado within 45 days. Agents not trained within 45 days were prohibited from making warrantless arrests.
  • Any ICE agents hired after May 12, 2026 were barred from making warrantless arrests until they completed the required training.
  • The court also ordered ICE to provide monthly documentation of training completion and arrest records to plaintiffs' attorneys, and to pay plaintiffs' attorney fees.
"ICE has continued to make warrantless arrests without individualized, pre-arrest probable cause determinations of flight risk in violation of [federal law] and the [preliminary injunction]," Jackson wrote.
mediaPlaceholder: 60-page court order detailing ICE's non-compliance and ordering mandatory training within 45 days
